FLOODING REPORTED IN RIPLEY, WITH AT LEAST 2 WATER RESCUES ONGOING INVOLVING 2 SEPERATE VEHICLES THAT HAVE DRIVEN INTO HIGH WATER AS OF 923 PM THURSDAY NIGHT. FIRST REPORT OF FLASH FLOODING WAS AT 832 PM. SEVERAL ROADS WERE CLOSED AND SEVERAL HOMES HAD WATER IN THEIR BASEMENTS DUE TO FLOODING. LAST REPORT WAS AT 1033 PM, AND FLOOD WATERS HAD RECEDED AS OF 1130 PM, SOME WITH THE HELP OF STORM DRAIN DEBRIS CLEARING, WHICH QUICKLY DROPPED THE WATER LEVEL IN ONE SPOT FROM HIP-DEEP TO ANKLE-DEEP.
METRO911 RECEIVED REPORTS OF HIGH WATER FLOODING E DUPONT AVE AND BARKER HOLLOW IN GLASGOW.
FINAL FLASH FLOOD IMPACTS OCCURRED AROUND 9PM WITH WATER THEN RECEDING SIGNIFICANTLY AFTER THEN.

CURRENTLY FOUR SECONDARY ROADS OFF OF US ROUTE 52 IN HANOVER ARE IMPASSABLE BECAUSE OF SMALL STREAMS LEAVING THEIR BANKS. LOWER MUZZLE RD, CANEBREAK RD, GARDEN GAP RD AND NORTH SPRINGS RD ARE ALL AFFECTED. NO HOMES OR BUSINESSES HAVE REPORTED ANY WATER DAMAGE.

RESULTS OF YESTERDAY MORNING’S STORM INCLUDE 4 DAMAGED BRIDGES, ONE DAMAGED CULVERT AFFECTING ACCESS TO 7 RESIDENCES AND 4 INCHES OF WATER IN A CHURCH ON NORTH SPRING ROAD IN HANOVER. ON LOWER MUZZLE ROAD IN HANOVER, TWO CULVERTS WASHED OUT AND ARE LODGED IN THE STREAM AND ONE CULVERT IS BADLY DAMAGED. 4 RESIDENCES ARE AFFECTED BY THESE DAMAGES.
